questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The Jury responsible for selecting the recipient of the 'Gandhi Peace Prize' typically includes which of the following members?

options.text: The President of India, the Prime Minister, and the Speaker of the Lok Sabha
The Prime Minister, the Chief Justice of India, and the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha
The Prime Minister, the Minister of Culture, and the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha
The Chief Justice of India, the Attorney General, and the Home Minister

questions.explanation: The selection of the Gandhi Peace Prize recipient is made by a high-level jury. Based on the provided context, the jury includes the Prime Minister of India, the Chief Justice of India, and the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha.
questions.takeaway: The Gandhi Peace Prize selection involves a cross-institutional jury representing the executive, judiciary, and the opposition.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Who amongst the following are members of the Jury to select the recipient of ‘Gandhi Peace Prize’? ... II. The Prime Minister of India, III. The Chief Justice of India, IV. The Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha.
